Arc Browser vs CleanShot X: Which Is Better?

Choosing between Arc Browser and CleanShot X? Here's a side-by-side comparison to help you decide.

Quick verdict: Choose Arc Browser if you need developers or designers. Choose CleanShot X if you prioritize developers or designers.

Arc Browser

PricingFree
Rating★★★★ 4.4/5

Key Features

  • Vertical sidebar tabs with visual tab previews
  • Spaces for separating browsing contexts (work, personal, project, etc.)
  • Split View for side-by-side page browsing
  • Easels for visual bookmarking, mood boarding, and idea organization
  • Boosts for customizing website appearance with CSS
  • Command palette (Cmd+T) for tab search, navigation, and actions

Pros

  • Vertical tabs are a genuinely better UI for tab management; you can read tab titles without squinting
  • Spaces let you separate work, personal, and project browsing with their own pinned tabs and profiles
  • Split view for side-by-side pages is built in and smooth to use
  • Easels for visual bookmarking and organizing ideas are a creative alternative to bookmarks
  • Boosts let you customize any website's appearance with CSS modifications (removing distractions, changing colors)

Cons

  • Mac and Windows only at this point — no mobile app beyond iPhone, no Linux support
  • Uses Chromium under the hood, so it inherits Chrome's resource usage and privacy concerns
  • Some features feel beta-quality; occasional crashes and performance hiccups happen
  • The different paradigm (no traditional tabs) can be disorienting for the first week
  • Auto-archiving tabs (Arc's feature that hides unused tabs) can lose track of tabs you meant to keep

CleanShot X

Pricing$29 one-time (Mac only)
Rating★★★★½ 4.6/5

Key Features

  • Advanced screenshot capture with scrolling, window, and region modes
  • Built-in annotation and markup tools
  • OCR text extraction from screenshots
  • Cloud upload with instant shareable links
  • Screen recording with optional cursor highlighting
  • History window with searchable screenshot archive

Pros

  • Scrolling screenshot capture is flawless — captures entire web pages, docs, or conversations in one shot
  • Annotation tools are excellent; arrows, text, blur, and highlights that look professional with no effort
  • OCR text extraction from screenshots is surprisingly fast and accurate
  • Cloud upload generates a shareable link instantly; saves the hassle of uploading to Imgur elsewhere
  • History window keeps a searchable archive of every screenshot you've ever taken

Cons

  • Mac only — Windows and Linux users need to look elsewhere (ShareX on Windows is a good alternative)
  • The video recording features, while useful, are basic compared to dedicated tools like Screen Studio
  • Some features (cloud upload, custom domains) require a paid subscription after the initial purchase
  • The floating capture menu can occasionally get in the way on smaller screens
  • No Linux version and no clear plans for one

Which Should You Choose?

Both Arc Browser and CleanShot X are excellent tools in their category. The right choice depends on your specific needs:

  • Choose Arc Browser if: Arc is the browser that reimagines what a browser can be. Instead of a horizontal tab bar that turns into a mess of tiny icons, Arc puts tabs vertically in a sidebar, organizes them into Spaces and Profiles, and adds a command palette for navigation. It's a controversial take — some people love it, some find it unnecessary — but it's the most innovative browser since the original Chrome.
  • Choose CleanShot X if: CleanShot X is the screenshot tool that Mac power users swear by. It replaces the built-in screenshot tool with a vastly superior experience: scrolling captures, OCR text extraction, annotation tools, cloud upload with shareable links, and a history window that stores everything you've captured. It's one of those tools that seems overpriced ($29 one-time) until you use it for a day and wonder how you lived without it.
We may earn a commission if you purchase through links. All opinions are our own.
